_provenance.source_pdf: Laukens, Dirk - The Beginner's Guide to Jazz Guitar.pdf

systems.name: Moveable-Root Chord System
systems.summary: The book's primary harmonic system treats every chord shape as a transposable unit anchored by a red-dot root. Five chord qualities—maj7, dominant 7, minor 7, half-diminished 7, and diminished 7—form the taxonomy. Because the root dot determines the chord's name ('root = chord name'), any shape can be slid to any fret to produce all 12 transpositions without learning new fingerings. These five qualities are organized around the ii–V–I–vi progression drawn from the major scale, and the system governs both chord construction and comping rhythm figures. The Roman-numeral notation (Chapter 7) supplies the labeling infrastructure the system presupposes, with capital numerals for major/dominant and small numerals for minor/diminished.

members.name: Major 7 Chord
members.summary: Tonic major quality; capital Roman numeral Imaj7.

members.name: Dominant 7 Chord
members.summary: Unstable dominant quality; capital Roman numeral V7.

members.name: Minor 7 Chord
members.summary: Minor quality; small Roman numeral ii or vi.

members.name: Half-Diminished 7 Chord
members.summary: Minor ii chord in minor key contexts; small Roman numeral with ø.

members.name: Diminished 7 Chord
members.summary: Symmetrical chord; only 2 moveable grips needed due to symmetry.

traversal_rules.name: Root = Chord Name Transposition Rule
traversal_rules.summary: Move any shape so the red-dot root lands on the desired pitch; the chord's name follows automatically.

references.quote_excerpt: the red note...tells you what the name of the chord is

traversal_rules.name: ii–V–I–vi Functional Progression Order
traversal_rules.summary: Chords are connected in the ii–V–I–vi sequence derived from the major scale.

references.quote_excerpt: These chords...are found in countless jazz standards

modification_rules.name: Tritone Substitution
modification_rules.summary: Replace any dominant 7 chord with another dominant 7 a tritone away; the shared 3rd and 7th preserve harmonic function.

references.quote_excerpt: play another 7th chord that occurs a tritone (#4 aka b5) away

modification_rules.name: Color Tone Embellishment
modification_rules.summary: Basic dominant 7 chord symbols on lead sheets may be expanded with 9ths, 13ths, and 6ths.

references.quote_excerpt: those chords can be embellished with 9ths, 13ths, 6ths, and other color tones

modification_rules.name: Diminished Symmetry Grip Reduction
modification_rules.summary: Because diminished 7 chords are symmetrical, only 2 moveable grips are required to cover all 12 keys.

references.quote_excerpt: You only need to learn 2 grips for diminished chords because diminished chords are symmetrical

modification_rules.name: Minor Blues Turnaround Substitution Variants
modification_rules.summary: The turnaround of a minor blues admits three standard substitution families.

references.quote_excerpt: variations include bVI-V-I, ii-V-I, or even biii-bVI/ii V/I

modification_rules.name: Sus Chord Dominant Delay
modification_rules.summary: Replace or precede a dominant chord with its sus version to add rhythmic tension and delay resolution.

references.quote_excerpt: Sus chords are a nice way to delay and bring extra motion to dominant chords

systems.name: Function-Assigned Scale and Arpeggio System
systems.summary: The book's second master system assigns specific scales and arpeggios to specific harmonic functions rather than to a key center. Joe Pass's aphorism 'When the chords change, you change' is the governing traversal rule.

traversal_rules.name: Chord-Specific Scale Change Rule
traversal_rules.summary: Scale selection must change with each chord change, not remain fixed on a single key center.

references.quote_excerpt: When the chords change, you change

traversal_rules.name: Arpeggio Proximity Voice-Leading Rule
traversal_rules.summary: Connect arpeggios by nearness of pitches rather than restarting from the root.

references.quote_excerpt: switch to the nearest note of the second arpeggio

traversal_rules.name: Arpeggio Free-Start and Free-Order Rule
traversal_rules.summary: Arpeggios may be started on any chord tone and played in any order.

references.quote_excerpt: Arpeggios can be started on any note and played in any order

traversal_rules.name: Minor Pentatonic Whole-Progression Bridge
traversal_rules.summary: On Summertime and similar minor progressions, the minor pentatonic or blues scale traverses the entire form.

references.topic: Pentatonic as bridge to jazz soloing
references.quote_excerpt: you can use the minor pentatonic or minor blues scale to solo over this entire progression

modification_rules.name: Tonic Harmonic Minor over V7alt
modification_rules.summary: Over V7alt in minor ii-V-I, use tonic harmonic minor (not the chord's root).

references.quote_excerpt: you use the tonic harmonic minor scale

modification_rules.name: Melodic Minor Raised-7th as Deliberate Tension
modification_rules.summary: The raised 7th of melodic minor over Im7 is a context-dependent expressive choice.

references.quote_excerpt: experiment with this scale...where you want to use that raised 7th

modification_rules.name: Major Blues Scale No-Clash Rule
modification_rules.summary: Lacking a 7th, the major blues scale can be applied over both dominant 7 and major 7 chords.

references.quote_excerpt: Because there is not 7th in this scale, it can be applied to both G7 and Gmaj7 chords

modification_rules.name: Phrygian Dominant as First-Choice V7 Scale
modification_rules.summary: In bebop, phrygian dominant is primary over V7/V7alt.

references.quote_excerpt: phrygian dominant scale is a first-choice sound when improvising over V7 and V7alt chords

modification_rules.name: 3-to-9 Arpeggio Substitution
modification_rules.summary: Dim7 arpeggio from the 3rd of any 7th chord outlines 3-5-b7-b9.

references.quote_excerpt: When playing a dim7 arpeggio from the 3rd of any 7th chord, you'll outline the 3-5-b7-b9 intervals

modification_rules.name: Approach Note Chromatic Resolution Rule
modification_rules.summary: Approach a chord tone from one fret below; the chromatic neighbor must resolve to a chord tone.

references.quote_excerpt: approach any note in an arpeggio by one fret below

modification_rules.name: Enclosure Decoration
modification_rules.summary: Surround the target chord tone with one fret above and one below before landing.

references.quote_excerpt: you play one fret above, then one fret below, then the chord tone

modification_rules.name: Side-Stepping Minor Pentatonic Pair
modification_rules.summary: Alternate two minor pentatonic scales a half step apart over a 7th chord.

references.quote_excerpt: Playing between two minor pentatonic scales over a 7th chord, a 1/2 step apart

modification_rules.name: b3 / 3 Mixing for Jazz-Blues Color
modification_rules.summary: Mix minor blues b3 with Mixolydian major 3rd.

references.topic: Mixing b3 and 3 for bluesy sound
references.quote_excerpt: Mixing b3 and 3 is often used by jazz musicians to create a bluesy sound

modification_rules.name: Lydian Dominant over Tritone Substitute
modification_rules.summary: On a tritone substitute chord, use the Lydian Dominant scale (= altered scale of the original chord).

references.topic: Lydian Dominant as tritone sub scale
references.quote_excerpt: B Lydian Dominant scale (= F Altered scale). B7 is the tritone substitute of F7

modification_rules.name: Harmonic Minor over Dom 7b9
modification_rules.summary: Use harmonic minor over a dominant 7 to produce a 7b9 color.

references.quote_excerpt: uses the G Harmonic Minor scale over D7, creating a 7b9 sound

systems.name: Comping Rhythm and Texture System
systems.summary: A smaller system governing how chords are deployed rhythmically in accompaniment, with four comping figures and rules on cutoff and metronome practice.

members.name: Freddie Green Rhythm
members.summary: Steady four-to-the-bar with accents on 2 and 4.

members.name: Anticipation
members.summary: Next bar's chord on the & of 4.

traversal_rules.name: Comping Rhythm Figure Cycling
traversal_rules.summary: After learning chord changes, vary between the four comping figures.

references.quote_excerpt: After you can play these chords and rhythms, change the rhythms

traversal_rules.name: Hi-Hat Ensemble Alignment
traversal_rules.summary: Beats-2-and-4 comping aligns with the drummer's hi-hat for ensemble swing.

references.quote_excerpt: line up with the hi-hat on the drum kit

modification_rules.name: Chord Cutoff Before Beats 2 and 4
modification_rules.summary: In Freddie Green rhythm, chords must be muted before they ring over onto beats 2 and 4.

references.quote_excerpt: cut the chords off so they don't ring over on beats 2 and 4

modification_rules.name: Metronome Requirement for Syncopated Figures
modification_rules.summary: Syncopated figures (anticipation, Charleston) must be practiced with a metronome.

references.quote_excerpt: work with a metronome and go slow until you're ready to speed this pattern

systems.name: Chord-Melody Texture System
systems.summary: A specialized system for arranging jazz standards as solo guitar chord-melody, with a three-layer architecture (bass / chord / melody) and named contrary-motion and Joe-Pass-split rules.

traversal_rules.name: Contrary Motion Voice Leading
traversal_rules.summary: Prefer contrary motion between bass and inner voices when moving between chords.

references.quote_excerpt: Contrary motion in voice leading sounds nice

traversal_rules.name: Joe Pass Bass/Treble Split with Chord Accent
traversal_rules.summary: Alternate bass note with top chord voicing; accent only the chord portion.

references.quote_excerpt: break up the chord into the bass note and the top 3 notes of the shape

modification_rules.name: Melody Confinement to Upper Strings
modification_rules.summary: Melody must be moved to the top two strings to allow chord voicings below.

references.quote_excerpt: melody has been moved to mostly the top 2 strings

modification_rules.name: Kenny Burrell Chord / Single-Note Mixing
modification_rules.summary: Within a chorus, intersperse three-note chord shapes with single-note blues-scale lines.

references.quote_excerpt: mixing chords and single-lines during each phrase of an improvised chorus
